steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: The sentence says historians were unable to ______ the authenticity of the letters and dismissed them as a hoax. If historians dismissed the letters as a hoax, it is because they could not confirm those letters were genuine. The blank needs a word meaning "confirm as authentic."

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'validate': means to confirm or verify as genuine. If historians could not validate authenticity, dismissing the letters as a hoax follows logically. ✓
  • Choice B — 'interpret': means to explain or make sense of. Failing to interpret authenticity does not logically lead to calling something a hoax — misuse of word meaning. ✗
  • Choice C — 'relate': means to tell or narrate to others. Historians not being able to relate the letters' authenticity is incoherent in context — the text says they shared their conclusion freely. ✗
  • Choice D — 'accommodate': means to give consideration to or make room for. Historians actively considered authenticity and rejected it, so "unable to accommodate" contradicts the passage. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
